Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/c8389860-712e-11e2-9241-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  2 tests failed: replay-single, sanity.

Patch Set 9:

Jinshan, any idea what's wrong with this patch? If we really want a HSM-aware client in 2.4, then we should consider landing this patch ...

Patch Set 9:

Even this patch is landed, we still can't make 2.4 clients to be HSM aware because MDT won't be notified if a released file will be read and written on client.

Based on this case, as what we discussed in HSM status meeting last week, we decided to land this feature along with file release feature. At that time we can test this patch completely.

Patch Set 9: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Will this cause problems for old clients, or would the MDS already block access to released files for old clients that don't understand HSM?

I'm a little bit reluctant to have _just_ lmm_stripe_count==0 to indicate a released file.  What about adding a LOV_PATTERN_RELEASED flag to make it very clear that this is not just a layout that was not allocated correctly?

Patch Set 9:

I agree, and I'd rather to have a new pattern code instead. It's not good to imply something by stripe count.

Good point on old clients. I think it's necessary for old clients to be blocked to access this released layout. What will be the expected behavior?

Patch Set 9:

For old client we can act as a LOV_DELAY_CREATE file

Patch Set 9:

Using a new stripe pattern was also my initial suggestion. When i last discussed this with Andreas, he was more in favor of using a null stripe count.
Anyway, let's use a new stripe pattern then. Jinshan, do you have some spare cycles to update the patch? TIA
Uploaded patch set 10.

Uploaded patch set 11.

Patch Set 11: (1 inline comment)
Patch Set 10: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 12:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 12:

What happened to idea of using a new flag in the layout to mark it explicitly released instead of just stripe_count == 0?

Patch Set 12:

Hi Andreas, I like to mark it as released file with an explicit flag too, but I can't find out which field in lov_mds_md should be used. I even thought about using a new lov pattern for released file but realized it would cause lots of extra work.

This patch introduces new feature so I don't know if it will be landed in 2.4. Otherwise it would be perfect to do it based on Alex's new layout.

Patch Set 12:

Jinshan, I would suggest to use a high bit flag in lmm_pattern to mark the file released.

Patch Set 12:

In that case, let's split the pattern field into two u8: one as original pattern and the other one as a flag. We won't have over 256 patterns in near future, will we?

Patch Set 12:

Sorry I remember it wrong, lmm_pattern has 32 bit, so I think it's okay to split it into two fields and have a new flag field

Patch Set 12:

I already consider lmm_pattern to be "flag-like" in that we will likely use combinations of pattern bits to define new patterns. We would likely want to have a "released" bit for any pattern to make it clear that the file is inaccessible due to HSM.

In any case, it makes sense to pick a high bit for this, since it gives us more flexibility for the future. Splitting into separate words is limiting in flexibility, and also causes issues with swabbing on big-endian systems.

Patch Set 12:

Such a flag already exist in HSM flags so I do not thinck we need to add it in lov ea (especially because there is no right place). If we want to add explicit knowledge, the RAID pattern is the best, if not possible now the stripecount==0 is a good workaround waiting for the RAID pattern solution.

Patch Set 12: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It should be fine to use something like:

 #define LOV_PATTERN_RELEASED 0x80000000

and we can decide later whether this is a flag or a number.  Could we get a new patch?

Patch Set 12:

Yes, I will do that definitely.
Uploaded patch set 13.

I thought the point was to check for LOV_PATTERN_RELEASED instead of stripe_count == 0?

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/93412ef8-a8c2-11e2-85b9-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  11 tests failed: sanity-hsm, runtests, mmp, lustre-rsync-test, sanity-quota, insanity, replay-ost-single, recovery-small, conf-sanity, replay-single, sanity.

Patch Set 13: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e542931c-a8cd-11e2-a141-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  2 tests failed: replay-single, sanity.

Patch Set 12:

I have a wording issue. For HSM feature a released file is a file without DATA in Lustre and with a valid archive.
Here the patch introduces the explicit not DATA state but it is not a valid release state for HSM. So I think we have to change the name here and keep release name for HSM.
What do you think of NODATA or SHELL or EMPTYSHELL

Patch Set 13:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/f5ff7b64-a8de-11e2-a141-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  3 tests failed: recovery-small, replay-single, sanity.

Patch Set 13:

We cannot any more use this lov without stripe state as a criteria to trigger a restore because it is a valid lustre state in which lustre will see only 0 in the file. Do you agree if we use HSM flags as a the criteria to trigger the restore from the client ?
NODATA file + archive flag => restore needed
NODATA file => read returns 0, write allocates objects and fill
Patch Set 13: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 13:

Hi JC, why can't a new pattern trigger(old pattern + flag can be considered as new pattern) restore of HSM files?

Sorry if I missed something

Patch Set 13:

Old pattern + flag does not change the issue. The pb is that we have to make a difference between a O_LOV_DELAY_CREATE file (no restore needed, as the one you use on your sanity test) and RELEASED file (restore needed). The only difference is the hsm archive file.
This is also why I suggest to change the name by NODATA

Patch Set 13:

JC, the case of O_LOV_DELAY_CREATE will be the same as today - nosy out will be created. The LOV_PATTERN_RELEASED flag would only be used for files that HSM has released.
Uploaded patch set 14.

Patch Set 14:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/14989/

Patch Set 14:

This is just a draft of using RELEASED flag with old layout. I realize this is error prone as stripe_count can't be used directly. It can't pass maloo test but I submitted it earlier to make sure you like it.

Patch Set 14: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/14989/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 14:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 14:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 14:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 14:

The last way to manage release file is ok but how do we do an import? (ie how do we create a file with pattern + release flag)

Patch Set 14:

"how do we create a file with pattern + release flag"

Take a look at sanity:test_229 and -H option of multiop which creates a released file for testing purpose.

Patch Set 14:

Sorry I missed V14 and forgot we can specify the raid pattern.
It is ok

Patch Set 14:

BTW, is it difficult to save original layout somewhere else so that we can have an independent pattern for released file, not just a flag?

Patch Set 14: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Not totally clear why the stripe count is set to zero for released files, but we'll see how this behaves in testing.

Patch Set 14:

Isn't  the HSM release process done in the kernel by the MDS code? It can set the pattern when it releases the file.

Patch Set 14:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e77bf8a4-aaed-11e2-a7ad-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  2 tests failed: recovery-small, sanity.

Patch Set 14:

Following this patch, we have to change llapi_hsm_import(), do we do this in this patch or do we make a separate JIRA/patch? In both case I can do it

Patch Set 14:

Nasf, please take a look at this patch to check if it will affect fsck. Long story in short, a new flag:LOV_PATTERN_RELEASED of lmm_pattern is defined within raid0 layout; if this flag is set, it has been released by HSM and there is no object for this file.

Patch Set 14:

"I have just found a bug hit at layout_swap after restore"

Is this newly added code, I didn't find there is this function in lod_lov.c

Patch Set 14:

Ooops, this code comes from our release patch ...

parents.subject: LU-3283 llite: check ll_prep_md_op_data() using IS_ERR()

commit.subject: LU-2482 layout: introduce new layout for released files
commit.message:
LU-2482 layout: introduce new layout for released files

Released files now have a standard layout (with generation, pool, ...)
and a stripe count 0 and lmm_pattern flag LOV_PATTERN_F_RELEASED.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Johann Lombardi <johann.lombardi@intel.com>
Change-Id: Id2c3b95fdd304f803d391281a86927f5c93cb3b4
